prompt:
A large 150 liter (roughly 40 gallon) rectangular glass goldfish aquarium on a sturdy stand in a bright living room, clearly long and spacious with plenty of horizontal swimming room. The water level is slightly lowered for maintenance, and a siphon hose runs from the tank down into a bucket on the floor, showing water being removed. One or two goldfish calmly swim among live plants and smooth river stones while a hang-on-back filter and heater remain running, clearly indicating a serious, full-sized goldfish setup rather than a tiny desktop tank.

Near-future solarpunk home environment with soft natural light from a nearby window, a few houseplants and simple eco-friendly décor in the background. Photorealistic 512×512 composition, no brand logos or legible labels. A bottle of water conditioner and a container of fresh replacement water are placed beside the bucket, and at most only a partial human hand or arm is visible holding the siphon, emphasizing the safe, routine partial water change process on a large, properly sized goldfish aquarium.
